title: plugin-agent-codex:plugin-agent
description: This spec defines the Codex agent plugin contract for install/uninstall behavior, install target directory, and exact frontmatter support so implementation and verification are deterministic.
context: This spec defines the Codex agent plugin contract for install/uninstall behavior, install target directory, and exact frontmatter support so implementation and verification are deterministic.

rules: The package MUST export `create(): AgentPlugin` as default or named export.

The implementation MUST return a valid `AgentPlugin` that:

- has `type: 'agent'`
- has a concrete package name and version
- implements `install(projectRoot, options)` and `uninstall(projectRoot, options)` with real filesystem behavior
- uses a stable frontmatter model for generated skill files

The plugin MUST install skills from `@specd/skills` and inject Codex-compatible frontmatter into markdown skill files during install.

Installation flow MUST:

1. load requested skills (or all skills when no filter is provided)
2. resolve frontmatter for each skill
3. prepend frontmatter to markdown files in the installed skill directories
4. return an `InstallResult` with installed and skipped entries

The Codex frontmatter contract MUST model and emit this exact supported set:

- `name` (required)
- `description` (required)

No other frontmatter keys are considered Codex-supported in this spec.
Skills MUST be installed to `.codex/skills/` under the provided project root.

constraints: The plugin MUST depend on @specd/skills for skill operations.
The plugin MUST implement concrete install and uninstall behavior.

scenarios.name: Exposes plugin factory
then: create() is available and returns an AgentPlugin

scenarios.name: Created plugin satisfies runtime contract
then: the returned plugin has type: 'agent', package name, and package version
AND the returned plugin exposes install(projectRoot, options) and uninstall(projectRoot, options) functions

scenarios.name: Install injects frontmatter and reports outcome
when: install(projectRoot, options) runs with a selected skill set
then: installed markdown skill files include Codex-compatible frontmatter prepended before content
AND the install result reports installed and skipped entries

scenarios.name: Codex emitter limits fields to supported keys
when: frontmatter is generated for a skill file
then: only name and description are emitted

scenarios.name: Skills are written under Codex directory
then: files are created only under .codex/skills/ within the target project root
